quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards! Their wings can rotate in a full circle, allowing them to hover in place and even fly upside down for short distances. The Pacific Northwest is home to Anna's hummingbirds, which stay here year-round even in winter.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals in the Salish Sea
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals are curious and playful neighbors in the waters around Port Angeles. Learn about their daily routines, how they communicate, and why they love basking on rocky shores.

medium_read.title: How Hungarian Embroidery Tells Stories Through Stitches
medium_read.teaser: Every region of Hungary has its own distinct embroidery style, passed down through generations. Discover the beautiful patterns and what they symbolize, from flowers that represent hope to colors that mark special celebrations.

long_read.title: Olympic National Park: A Wilderness Like No Other
long_read.teaser: From temperate rainforests to alpine peaks and wild coastline, Olympic National Park contains remarkable ecosystems. This beautiful exploration takes you through the park's history, its incredible biodiversity, and the efforts to preserve it for future generations.

port_angeles.title: Black-tailed Deer at Ediz Hook
port_angeles.description: Ediz Hook is a wonderful spot to see Columbian black-tailed deer grazing peacefully near the water. Early morning and evening are the best times to spot them. The long sandbar also offers stunning views of the Olympic Mountains and the Strait of Juan de Fuca.

puzzle_1.question: A ruby-throated hummingbird's heart beats about ___ times per minute while flying.
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's exactly right! Isn't that amazing? Their tiny hearts work incredibly hard to power their flight.
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! The answer is 1,200 beats per minute — that's about 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those tiny hearts beat an astounding 1,200 times per minute! That's what gives them the energy to hover and fly in all directions.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To support such a fast metabolism, hummingbirds must eat about half their body weight in sugar every day, visiting hundreds of flowers. At night, they enter a hibernation-like state called torpor to conserve energy.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is such a beautiful word for such a beautiful creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó' — doesn't it sound lovely? (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and méh means bee.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: The word 'pillangó' likely comes from the way butterflies flutter and dance through the air. Hungary is home to over 200 species of butterflies, including the stunning swallowtails that visit garden flowers.

puzzle_3.question: What year was Olympic National Park officially established?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Yes, 1938! President Franklin Roosevelt established it to protect the magnificent wilderness we treasure today. What a gift to future generations!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Olympic National Park was established in 1938 by President Franklin Roosevelt. It preserves nearly a million acres of diverse ecosystems, from rainforest to mountain to coast!
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The area was first protected as Mount Olympus National Monument in 1909, but it took decades of advocacy to create the full national park. In 1981, it was designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
